must_detect.matched_text: Selective disclosure risk: the unannounced May trading update and unaudited synergies model shown to two wall-crossed funds under NDA are MNPI; any further approach must follow the Exchange’s guidance on fair disclosure and be logged on the insider list.

Subtle risk: the draft press release for “Project Kestrel” contains bracketed placeholders revealing earnings accretion assumptions; although not shared externally, screenshots in the data room preview pane may inadvertently disclose them—tighten permissions.

Confidential – Internal Compliance Note: Arclight Pacific Holdings Ltd (Mainboard-listed on the LionCity Exchange (LCX)); counsel: Straits Harbour Legal LLP.
As at 12 June 2026, non-public items include the draft SPA with Novaterra Robotics Pte. Ltd. and the indicative exchange ratio range; these are reasonably expected to be price-sensitive to Arclight and constitute issuer-relative MNPI.
Public reference: the LCXNet announcement dated 15 April 2026 on Arclight’s strategic options review is already generally available and should not be treated as MNPI; reliance on that document alone is permissible for external communications.
Selective disclosure risk: the unannounced May trading update and unaudited synergies model shown to two wall-crossed funds under NDA are MNPI; any further approach must follow the Exchange’s guidance on fair disclosure and be logged on the insider list.
Blackout windows: Arclight is in a closed period from quarter-end until release of results via LCXNet; dealing by directors and employees is prohibited absent pre-clearance and the window will be extended event-specifically until the SPA is signed and announced without delay in accordance with the Listing Manual.
PDPA: the data room contains employee rosters, personal emails, compensation bands, and interview notes for key personnel (e.g., Ms. Livia Tan, Company Secretary; Dr. Omar Chen, CTO), which are personal data; collection relies on the business asset transaction exception and must be minimised, with redaction of national ID scans and removal of unnecessary identifiers.
Employment integration: draft retention packages for Mr. Teo Wai Seng and Ms. Renée Koh include salary step-ups and option grants; these documents contain both PDPA-covered information and MNPI and must not be circulated beyond the deal team.
Capital markets: the contemplated private placement to finance completion has not been cleared with the Authority; pre-sounding must be limited to investors willing to be wall-crossed, with records of timing and acknowledgements kept for LCX review.
Leak protocols: if pricing or counterparty identity appears in media ahead of announcement, request a trading halt and make an immediate clarifying LCXNet release; otherwise, maintain confidentiality and target after-market disclosure timing per Exchange practice.
Non-MNPI references: Citystate Business’ October 2024 profile on Arclight’s factory expansion and a routine vendor notice that Golden Helix Logistics will shift to weekend operations are stale or non-issuer operational updates and not price-sensitive.
Subtle risk: the draft press release for “Project Kestrel” contains bracketed placeholders revealing earnings accretion assumptions; although not shared externally, screenshots in the data room preview pane may inadvertently disclose them—tighten permissions.
Records: maintain an up-to-date insider list with timestamps for all recipients, including external advisors at Straits Harbour Legal LLP and LionBridge Capital Partners Pte. Ltd.; PDP
